external_narratives.summary: Dartmoor artefacts database entry for the B-17 Flying Fortress crash at Tiger's Marsh on Christmas Day 1943. The aircraft (serial 42-37869) had landed at RAF St Eval in Cornwall after a meteorological reconnaissance sortie and was departing for its base at Cheddington, Hertfordshire.
external_narratives.key_details: Serial 42-37869. Pilot attempted to maintain visual contact with the ground on a north-easterly track but entered cloud hanging over the valley. The aircraft struck the hillside, bounced up the slope, and caught fire. The impact area is permanently blackened — the grass has never grown back.

external_narratives.summary: Accident database entry for Boeing B-17G Flying Fortress 42-37869, recording the crash on 25 December 1943 at Tiger's Marsh, Dartmoor, during a transit flight from RAF St Eval.
external_narratives.key_details: Five crew killed, three survived with injuries. Survivors included crew members who were rescued from the remote moorland location. Crash occurred on Christmas Day 1943.

external_narratives.summary: Narrative account of Dartmoor's WW2 crash sites including the B-17 at Tiger's Marsh. Describes the memorial stone and the lasting physical scar on the landscape where the aircraft burned.
external_narratives.key_details: Crew included Ernest H Patterson, Raymond A Coates, Richard T Nearly, Sherwood R Renner, Samuel L Craig, Mario A Panitti, Albeny T Blanchard, and Basil Brown (RAF liaison). Memorial stone marks the crash site.
